Literature Digest

May 18 – Jun 01, 2026  ·  Chuong Lab, CU Boulder
955 scanned
15 preprints
20/20 journals
keyword + AI

AI Summary

This digest is headlined by three strong TE biology papers. Liu et al. demonstrate that R-loops accumulate at transposable elements in replication-quiescent oocytes and are required for TE transcriptional silencing. Feschotte's Comment in NRG synthesizes mounting evidence that TEs drive evolutionary innovation — a direct articulation of the lab's central hypothesis — and is essential reading. Dayama et al. extend TE reactivation to aging and neurodegeneration, documenting TE RNA dysregulation in Huntington's and Parkinson's brains.

On the immunity and RNA front, Bodelón et al. apply Oxford Nanopore long-read sequencing to primary human monocytes, finding immune activation drives extensive alternative splicing toward novel functional isoforms — paralleling the lab's Nanopore-based TE-derived isoform discovery. Weixler et al. show DHX36 limits IFN antiviral responses by resolving G4 structures in IRF3/IFN mRNAs. Li et al. find cGAS suppresses hypoxia signaling via autophagic HIF-1a/2a degradation. SPAC-seq enables genome-wide CRISPR perturbation screens in intact tissue.

Curated Highlights 24 papers

Top Journal Papers 20
R-loop homeostasis at transposable elements safeguards transcriptional silencing in replication-quiescent oocytes.
May 29, 2026 · Liu Shao-Yuan, Sha Qian-Qian et al. · Nature Communications
R-loops accumulate at transposable elements in replication-quiescent oocytes and are required for TE transcriptional silencing. → article
A reference genome for cultured Drosophila ovarian somatic cells enables studies of transposon and piRNA biology
May 22, 2026 · Unknown · Genome Biology
A new Drosophila OSC reference genome enables accurate TE annotation and improved study of nuclear PIWI-piRNA silencing. → article
Native long-read RNA sequencing of human monocytes reveals activation-induced alternative splicing toward functional isoforms.
May 29, 2026 · Bodelón Alejandra, van Loosdregt Jorg et al. · Nature Communications
Native long-read sequencing reveals activation-induced alternative splicing generating novel functional isoforms in primary human monocytes. → article
Transposable element small RNAs and large RNAs in aging brains and implications in Huntington's and Parkinson's disease.
May 28, 2026 · Dayama Gargi, Lau Nelson C et al. · Genome Research
TE small and large RNAs are dysregulated in aging human and mouse brains in Huntington and Parkinson disease. → article
Ancient but dynamic: structural expansion, massive gene duplication, and transposable element colonization in a supergene controlling ant social organization.
May 27, 2026 · Boulain Hélène, Chapuisat Michel et al. · Molecular Biology and Evolution
A 30-million-year-old ant supergene underwent massive gene duplication and TE colonization while retaining social organization control. → article

Transposable elements as catalysts of evolutionary innovation
May 28, 2026 · Cedric Feschotte · Nature Reviews Genetics
TEs repeatedly introduce regulatory and coding sequences that drive evolutionary innovation across diverse lineages. → article
